articles.title: With North America and Europe Tour Dates to Come, Keli Holiday Has ‘Hunkered Down’ With His Next Album - Billboard
articles.snippet: # With North America and Europe Tour Dates to Come, Keli Holiday Has ‘Hunkered Down’ With His Next Album. Holiday had the honors of opening the ARIAs with a performance of his viral smash “Dancing2.” Just Holiday, his hair teased like a mane, chest out, staring down the barrel of national TV cameras. He’s a first-time nominee for the APRA Music Awards, including the coveted song of the year for “Dancing2,” which took flight on TikTok, where fans clamored to its message of love, connection and positivity. This new chapter for Keli Holiday is a “representative of love,” he tells *Billboard* over Zoom. He’s the alter-ego of Adam Hyde, who alongside Reuben Styles is co-founder of Peking Duk, the multi-platinum, ARIA Award-winning electronic duo which has accumulated more than 400 million streams across such songs as “High,” “Take Me Over,” “Stranger” and “Fire.” The duo’s debut album, *Paradise*, is due out Aug. 14 and features the cut “Thrills” with Rico Nasty.

articles.title: 'Michael' Clip of Jaafar Jackson's 'Billie Jean' Performance Slammed by Fans: 'F--king Atrocious' - TheWrap
articles.snippet: “Literally the least inspired cinematography I’ve ever seen,” one critic writes on X. A new clip from the highly anticipated Michael Jackson biopic, titled “Michael,” was released Wednesday and featured star Jaafar Jackson’s take on “Billie Jean.” And, after being viewed over 8 million times, the consensus for the sneak peek was resoundingly negative. In the clip, Jackson, the real-life nephew of the late King of Pop, can be seen wearing his uncle’s signature white glove and black bedazzled jacket while singing the 1983 hit, “Billie Jean.” The scene is a recreation of the late Jackson’s famed performance for “Motown 25: Yesterday, Today, Forever,” where he debuted his famous moonwalk move for the first time. “Literally the least inspired cinematography I’ve ever seen,” one critic wrote in response to the clip’s release. “Michael,” which is set to hit theaters on April 24, has faced its fair share of drama leading up to the release, including millions in reshoots, a scrapped final act, a delayed release and more.

articles.title: YouTuber Jesser Launches Parent Company, Preps Business Expansion - ADWEEK
articles.snippet: # YouTuber Jesser Launches Parent Company, Preps Business Expansion. ## The media company, which generates an eight-figure revenue, has content and product debuts coming later this year. On Friday, the YouTube creator Jesse “Jesser” Riedel announced the formation of JesserCo., a new parent company that will unite his growing portfolio of businesses under a single structure. The 27-year-old Jesser will remain founder, while Zach Miller, who previously served as president of Bucketsquad, Jesser’s apparel brand, has been elevated to president of JesserCo. The reorganization brings together two main operating businesses: Jesser Media, the newly named home for his content operation, and Bucketsquad, the basketball lifestyle and apparel brand. Jesser Media reaches more than 45 million subscribers and followers, primarily through his YouTube channel, and has generated more than 10 billion views across platforms, according to the company. articles.title: A Belieber's Review of Justin Bieber's Coachella 2026 Set - Vogue Philippines
articles.snippet: Two hours before Justin Bieber began his 2026 Coachella set last night, I found myself inching closer and closer towards the main stage, where the Canadian pop star and late millennial icon was set to perform at 11:25 p.m. Some diehard Beliebers—Justin’s fiercest fans—were already camped out near the front barricades. Bieber began totally alone on the stage with one of his new tracks, “All I Can Take.” In true J.B. fashion, he emerged wearing a large oversized pink hoodie from his own brand SKYLRK, and baggy shorts by Lu’u Dan. Then came other tracks from his *Swag* and *Swag II* albums: he sang “Speed Demon,” “Go Baby,” “Everything Hallelujah”—with the latter track, the camera panned to his wife Hailey Bieber in the crowd, as she watched on lovingly and sang along. Nearing the end of the show, Bieber shed his pink hoodie and shades to rock a simple black cutoff tee—returning to perform more stripped-down *Swag* tracks like “Yukon” and “Daisies.” More special guests emerged, including Tems, Wizkid, Dijon, the Kid LAROI, and Mk.gee.

articles.title: Kehlani Teams Up With Missy Elliott For Aaliyah-Tributing ‘Back and Forth’ Single - Billboard
articles.snippet: It finds Kehlani in their signature lights-low R&B groove, with Elliott kicking thing off with some bold bars, boasting, “You know it’s some hot s–t/ Let me say what I’m gon’ say, let me stay on the topic/ Girl, woman, I do what I wanna do/ Why you trippin’ when I come right home to you?” over the song’s languorous beat before Kehlani steps in on the whispery first verse. The pre-chorus amps up the heat, with Kehlani singing, “And by-y-y-y-y/ By doing this later, I do us a favor / And by-y-y-y-y/ Don’t ruin my makeup, be there when you wake up,” before dipping into the singsongy chorus that pays homage to Elliott’s legendary late friend and collaborator Aaliyah’s 1994 debut hit single “Back & Forth.”. The new song comes after an epic run for Kehlani in 2025, which included the crossover anthem “Folded,” which landed the singer several chart milestones, with the track peaking at No. 6 on the Billboard Hot 100, marking her first top 10 on that singles chart.
articles.summary: 此新聞資訊與周杰倫無關，主要報導Kehlani與Missy Elliott合作新歌並向Aaliyah致敬。

articles.title: University of Wisconsin Board of Regents votes to fire system President Jay Rothman - Wisconsin Examiner
articles.snippet: # University of Wisconsin Board of Regents votes to fire system President Jay Rothman. The University of Wisconsin Board of Regents voted unanimously to fire UW President Jay Rothman on Tuesday, ending his tenure as the leader of UW system campuses. The University of Wisconsin Board of Regents voted unanimously to fire UW President Jay Rothman on Tuesday, ending his tenure as the leader of UW system campuses and launching the process of finding a new leader. In a statement, the Board of Regents said that Rothman had “worked hard to bring the best to the campuses, students, faculty and staff” and those efforts are appreciated, but “despite these accomplishments, based on the annual performance review and subsequent discussions, the Board has lost confidence in President Rothman’s ability to lead the UWs moving forward.”. WMC said Rothman has helped to enhance the UW’s reputation and “that reputation will be severely tarnished by the Board of Regents if it votes to remove Jay from his position without justification.”.
articles.summary: University of Wisconsin Board of Regents votes to fire system President Jay Rothman - Wisconsin Examiner

articles.title: Jin: ‘My Biggest Dream Has Been to Go on Tour With BTS’ - Rolling Stone
articles.snippet: In our cover-story interview, BTS’ powerhouse singer talks about his rock-influenced solo work, loving Coldplay, and what really matters to him. Even in a remarkably fan-centered group, his attentiveness to the feelings of ARMY is intense — he saw his solo EPs and shows primarily as a way to keep fans entertained during the group’s break, and he pushed to extend BTS’ tour. He’s also the group’s designated rocker, even collaborating with Coldplay, though he hasn’t had time to see as many bands as he’d like. I tend to like rock-adjacent genres, and have always wanted to include that sound in a solo album. **Your solo tour had rock energy and a new approach — it was very different from what you do with BTS. How did that experience carry forward into both making the album and when you’re going to be touring with BTS?**. I feel like it takes those rock elements that you love and that you use so well.**.

articles.snippet: # Jason Bembry’s ‘My Friends’ Embraces Self-Confidence. **Jason Bembry** returns with new single ‘My Friends’. **Jason Bembry** uses music as a means to explore his emotions, processing some complex feelings in the process. Out now, new single ‘My Friends’ finds Jason Bembry revelling in self-confidence. The studio gloss seems to amplify the emotional meaning of the lyrics, with the 29-year-old using the single as a means to express self-worth. Merging the introspection of **James Blake** with the dancefloor expression of **Disclosure**, ‘My Friends’ spotlights Jason Bembry’s multi-faceted musicality. *‘My Friends’ is all about the isolation and self-confidence that comes from feeling othered by people you thought were your friends. Jordan hit CinemaCon to showcase his latest directorial effort: a slick remake of “The Thomas Crown Affair.”. The annual convention of movie theater owners got a sneak peek at Jordan’s project — the third big-screen take on the story of a billionaire whose idle hands lead him to orchestrate major art thefts. Distributor Amazon MGM Studios cold opened their CinemaCon presentation with the trailer, showcasing Jordan in all his glossy leading man glory. The trailer played through the roof, which you can partly attribute to perfect timing — Jordan hasn’t been seen since his incredible best actor win for “Sinners.”. articles.title: Every Record Justin Bieber and SKYLRK Broke Before Coachella Weekend 2 - Newsweek
articles.snippet: # Every Record Justin Bieber and SKYLRK Broke Before Coachella Weekend 2. While the true triumph was that Bieber staged an evocative, genuine set, the hard data backs up that he and his SKYLRK brand also pulled off the most successful Coachella takeover by every available measure. Various outlets have reported that Bieber's reported $10 million for headlining Coachella is the richest earnings of any artist in the festival's history. Bieber also generated the highest ticket demand and the priciest tickets sold, while his 90-minute performance became the most viewed in Coachella history. Bieber and Neima Khaila co-founded SKYLRK, which officially launched last July. Bieber wore nothing but blue silk SKYLRK boxers and black SKYLRK socks while performing a stripped-back version of "YUKON" at the 2026 Grammys on Feb. 1, and he sported a SKYLRK outfit on the Coachella stage last weekend. ## The MAGA Trump Divorce, Six Months Later | Opinion.

articles.snippet: # Tenille Townes Releases Introspective, Self-Produced New Album ‘The Acrobat’. **TENILLE TOWNES****RELEASES INTROSPECTIVE, SELF-PRODUCED NEW ALBUM*****THE ACROBAT***. Friday, April 10 – Today, acclaimed singer-songwriter **Tenille Townes** released her highly anticipated new album ***The Acrobat***. The song also gained support from media with Holler describing the tune as *“a quietly devastating song that confronts the emotional cost of loving someone at the expense of yourself.”* PopMatterspraised “we could use a little more,” stating how the track’s “*uncompromising quietude demonstrates how loud the world has become”*and on title track “the acrobat” featuring Lori McKenna*,*Music Row applauded the *“starkly compelling artistry where you hang on every line.”*No Depressionhas already dubbed the record *“a terrific act of creative transformation.”*. Whether on record or on stage, Townes continues to create what she calls a “safe space” for listeners.Her live shows balance moments of quiet reflection with joy and release, grounded in the belief that music can help people feel less alone.*“The greatest measure of success for me is the stories people share about how a song helped them,”*she says.

articles.title: Isaia Huron confronts love, projection, and self-definition on ‘Mr. Lovebomb’ [Interview] - EARMILK
articles.snippet: Alternative R&B, soul, and pop singer, songwriter, and producer **Isaia Huron** has released his new record ***Mr. Lovebomb***, a title that feels as much like a societal critique as it does a personal moniker, reclaiming and owning a term often loaded with projection. More on his new record, Huron talks about how there was a particular song that came together early on that helped define the direction of *Mr. Lovebomb*: “The first song I made was “Side Slider," which is obviously the last song, but I needed to try to build a story that led up to this point of the main character having a moment of clarity of why he feels justified to cheat on his girl through the lens of uncovering why he's broken. Huron continues: ‘These guys have made it so easy for me to produce a record that, in essence, feels cohesive sonically and makes way for the story to shine through.

articles.title: Justin Bieber headlines Coachella with nostalgia-fuelled set - BBC
articles.snippet: # Justin Bieber headlines Coachella with nostalgia-fuelled set. Justin Bieber made a nostalgia-fuelled return to the major stage as Coachella's much-anticipated headline act. It marked the star's biggest live show in four years, having cancelled his Justice world tour following health issues. Dressed in a hoodie and shorts on a stripped-back stage, Bieber was joined by musical guests including The Kid Laroi, Wizkid, Tems and Dijon. The star's wife Hailey Bieber was spotted in the crowd, alongside celebrity couple Timothée Chalamet and Kylie Jenner. Bieber cancelled his world tour after revealing he was suffering from facial paralysis, after being diagnosed with Ramsay Hunt syndrome - a complication from contracting shingles - in 2022. His Coachella set marked a starker departure from the previous night's headline act, Sabrina Carpenter, whose Hollywood-themed gig included costume changes, choreography and elaborate stage design. ## Bieber cancels remaining Justice world tour dates. ## Justin and Hailey Bieber announce birth of first child.

On April 5, Hailey posted an Instagram carousel showing Jack playing with her newly launched Rhode pimple stickers and Justin holding Jack on his lap while watching his 2011 "Never Say Never" documentary. On Jake Shane's "Therapuss" Season 3 premiere, Hailey Bieber revealed that Jack started saying, "Baby, oh, Baby, oh" because he had been listening to his dad's seminal hit "Baby." articles.title: Zayn Kicks Back With ‘Konnakol’: Stream It Now - Billboard
articles.snippet: # Zayn Kicks Back With ‘Konnakol’: Stream It Now. The former One Direction singer will support the new release with several major TV appearances, and his biggest solo tour yet. The British pop singer is back with his fifth studio album, *Konnakol* (via Mercury Records/Island Records U.K.), a collection that houses the previously-released recording “Sideways” and lead single “Die For Me.”. *Konnakol* is Zayn’s first since 2024’s *Room Under the Stairs*, which peaked at No. 15 on the Billboard 200, and No. 3 on the U.K.’s Official Albums Chart. Zayn co-produced the new LP with Malay (Frank Ocean, Lorde), extending a working relationship that dates back to his debut solo album *Mind of Mine* (2016) and its followup *Icarus Falls* (2018). He made the best possible start with his first single “Pillowtalk,” which led the Billboard Hot 100 and the Official U.K. Singles Chart.

articles.title: Justin Bieber’s Skylrk Breaks Coachella Merch Sales Records - Vogue
articles.snippet: # Justin Bieber’s Skylrk Breaks Coachella Merch Sales Records. Via his brand Skylrk, Bieber sold $5.04 million worth of product over weekend one of Coachella, beating the festival’s previous two-weekend record of $1.7 million across both weekends, the brand confirmed exclusively to *Vogue Business*. Bieber did have a leg up via an additional sales outlet: on top of the Skylrk-produced ‘Swag’ merch available alongside other artists’ merch in the artist merch tent, the brand also sold product at the Skylrk Shop, located next to its Skylrk Oasis, a respite space with shade and cool mist, which was open to all festival attendees. Producing merch under the Skylrk brand generated strong media impact value (MIV) for the brand, achieving $2.3 million to date in MIV, according to Launchmetrics. Bieber officially founded Skylrk less than a year ago, in July 2025, after the artist began teasing the clothing brand back in December 2023. Throughout 2024, Bieber and his wife Hailey Bieber wore the brand consistently, before Bieber officially debuted the Skylrk site in 2025.

articles.title: Jessie Ware Sets Major International ‘Superbloom’ Tour - Billboard
articles.snippet: Ahead of her new album release, Jessie Ware announces a trans-Atlantic tour that's said to be her biggest to date, and includes her first-ever U.K. arena run. The British pop artist’s trans-Atlantic tour is said to be her biggest to date, and includes her first-ever U.K. arena run. The *Superbloom Tour* gets underway in Toronto on Oct. 6, and includes stops in Toronto, New York’s Radio City Music Hall, Washington DC, Chicago, San Francisco and Los Angeles’ Greek Theatre, before heading to Mexico City’s Teatro Metropolitan. According to a statement, the new collection “erupts into a glittering rush of Studio 54-inflected groove-pop, exploring themes of pleasure, intimacy, connection and transformation,” and houses the previously-released singles “I Could Get Used To This,” “Ride,” and “Automatic.”. I’m not the most by-the-book ‘pop star’, but I do like to play with dress-up, glamor, and fun, While I love dance music, I wanted to dig deeper with this record; to connect with real relationships and appreciate the love I have, and the fears I have of losing it.”. Oct. 8 — Radio City Music Hall, New York, NY.

articles.snippet: Bruno Mars unlocks the ultimate feel good show at tour opener. # Bruno Mars unlocks the ultimate feel good show at tour opener. ## Bruno Mars has history in Las Vegas. That Mars kicked off the nearly 80-date worldwide outing – his first stadium tour and his return to the road after almost a decade – in Las Vegas is more than happenstance. A few hours before Mars hit the Allegiant Stadium stage for the first of two sold-out shows Friday, April 10, he was bestowed with an honor not even the Queen of Las Vegas, Celine Dion, can boast – the official naming of his own street (Bruno Mars Drive) near the home of that long-term residency. While .Paak is on the tour to present an opening set as DJ Pee .Wee, he also vaulted out to perform a quartet of Silk Sonic tunes with Mars, including the funk-hip-hop-hybrid “777” and “the song that started this whole thing,” Mars said, the bedroom seducer “Leave the Door Open.”. ## Why Bruno Mars is a musical unicorn.

articles.title: Dave Chappelle stands firm through backlash and considers revisiting ‘Chappelle’s Show’ - Greenwich Time
articles.snippet: “I’ve had a lot of support from my people,” said Chappelle, an Emmy and Grammy winner. “Sometimes people will attach things to your voice that don’t necessarily have anything to do with you,” he said. Even as he reflects on his own career, Chappelle said he’s paying close attention to the next generation of comedians. “I love what he does,” Chappelle said before drawing a distinction between digital success and stand-up. “One of the worst things that can happen to a comedian is becoming successful before they get good,” Chappelle said. Chappelle remains active onstage, including upcoming performances tied to the Netflix Is a Joke Fest in Los Angeles in May. Back home, he continues to perform at his own comedy club — which used to be a firehouse — in Yellow Springs, where he has hosted surprise sets and brought in high-profile guests, including Travis Scott, Lizzo, Wyclef Jean, Marsha Ambrosius, Clipse, 50 Cent and Christopher Cross.

articles.title: Sitting in a jail cell, alone and hopeless, a man's life is suddenly changed - WXXI News
articles.snippet: # Sitting in a jail cell, alone and hopeless, a man's life is suddenly changed. When Jay was 22 years old, he was a self-described loner. And so I started making poor decisions," Jay said. "The next night I made a plan of how to do it, and I did it," Jay remembered. Jay made a plan to end his life. Jay had grown up religious, but had stopped going to church years before. He said yes, and listened as the man began to pray. "I wish I could tell you that I remember the [exact] words that he said to me, but what I remember is that his words landed with me, and instead of wanting my life to be over, suddenly I saw hope," Jay said. The interaction happened nearly ten years ago, but it was a pivotal moment in Jay's life, and one he thinks about all the time. Jay says that he wishes he could meet that man again and express his appreciation.

articles.title: Justin Bieber Goes Heavy On ‘Swag’ In Much-Anticipated Coachella Headlining Set - The Hollywood Reporter
articles.snippet: # Justin Bieber Goes Heavy On ‘Swag’ In Much-Anticipated Coachella Headlining Set. Bieber brought out The Kid Laroi to perform their smash hit stay, with other guests including Wizkid, Tems, Dijon and Mk.gee. Justin Bieber played his biggest concert in years as he headlined Coachella on Saturday night, delivering an intimate concert that leaned heavy on his *Swag* era and had Bieber mining through old YouTube clips on his laptop to take fans back to the early days. Kicking off at about 11:30 p.m., Bieber spent much of the first chunk of his set focused around his *Swag* era, opening with “All I Can Take” before going into “Speed Demon,” “First Place,” and “Go Baby.” He stuck with *Swag* songs until he brought out his first surprise guest of the evening, with The Kid Laroi coming out to perform their 2021 smash hit “Stay.”.

articles.title: Oasis Wins Major Tour of the Year Honor at 2026 Pollstar Awards - Variety
articles.snippet: # Oasis’ Comeback Outing Wins ‘Major Tour of the Year’ Prize at Pollstar Awards, as Kendrick Lamar/SZA, Benson Boone and the Weeknd Also Score Top Honors. Other top winners in genre-based categories included Metallica (for rock tour of the year), Kendrick Lamar/SZA (hip-hop tour of the year), the Weeknd (R&B tour of the year), Benson Boone (pop tour of the year), Bad Bunny (Latin tour of the year), Adam Sandler (comedy tour of the year), and Chris Stapleton and Lainey Wilson (who tied for country tour of the year). Among the awards going to individuals were the award for promoter of the year, which went to Live Nation’s Arthur Fogel, a bit of good news amid a tough day for that company, plus Barclay Center’s Laurie Jacoby as venue executive of the year, Red Light’s Coran Capshaw as personal manager of the year, Shore Fire’s Rebecca Shapiro as publicist of the year, CAA’s Allison McGregor as marketing executive of the year, and another CAA honoree, Darryl Eaton, as agent of the year.

articles.title: Jerry Seinfeld, Weird Al Yankovic to Headline Just For Laughs 2026 - The Hollywood Reporter
articles.snippet: Jerry Seinfeld and Weird Al Yankovic are returning to their comedy roots as headliners for the *Just For Laughs* comedy festival in Montreal in July, organizers said Wednesday. After directing his first movie, *Unfrosted,* for Netflix, Seinfeld will return to Montreal for its 2026 edition to perform his solo show, *Jerry Seinfeld Live*, on July 23 in the Place des Arts concert hall. ### Netflix Is a Joke Festival Sets 2026 Lineup: See Who Is Performing in Los Angeles in May](https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/tv/tv-news/netflix-is-a-joke-festival-2026-lineup-schedule-los-angeles-1236478031/). And Yankovic, who first played at JFL in Montreal in 1990, will perform his own solo show, *Weird Al Yankovic: Bigger and Weirder Tour,* for free on July 25 in the outdoor Place des Festivals venue. JFL organizers also announced their first wave of hosts for twice-nightly galas, including YouTube star Gabriel “Fluffy” Iglesias, *Trainwreck* actor Mike Birbiglia, Atsuko Okatsuka, whose second TV stand-up special streamed on Hulu and Disney+, and Gerry Dee, the *Animal Control* star.

articles.title: 6LACK Shares New Single ‘Sunday Again’ Featuring 2 Chainz - Billboard
articles.snippet: # 6LACK Shares New Single ‘Sunday Again’ Featuring 2 Chainz. 6LACK is back with new music. The Atlanta singer-rapper released “Sunday Again” featuring 2 Chainz on Friday, alongside a companion music video directed by Levi Turner, as the latest preview of his forthcoming album *Love Is the New Gangsta*, due May 22 via LVRN/Interscope Records. The video — which also features cameos from artists Mereba, umi, Quin and slimwav — sees 6LACK and 2 Chainz surrounded by friends and family in what the artist describes as a moment of community and serenity, reflecting the album’s central theme of universal love. The new album follows 6LACK’s 2023 effort *Since I Have a Lover*, which earned a Grammy nomination for Best Progressive R&B Album and received widespread critical praise from outlets including NPR, Pitchfork, Rolling Stone and Complex. His 2018 follow-up *East Atlanta Love Letter* peaked at No. 3 on the Billboard 200 and hit No. 1 on the Top R&B Albums chart.
articles.summary: 此新聞報導主要關於歌手6LACK發布新單曲《Sunday Again》並預告即將推出的專輯，內容與周杰倫無直接關聯。

articles.title: Jackson legacy on the line as ‘Michael’ biopic divides the family once more - New York Post
articles.snippet: # Jackson legacy on the line as ‘Michael’ biopic divides the family once more. “In what should be a great time for this family, a time when everyone really gets a chance to not only rally around [ailing family matriarch] Katherine and the memory of Michael, but to celebrate Jaafar, there is still division,” a family source told The Post. On one side, three of the Jackson 5 family band which launched Michael’s career: Jermaine, 71, Jackie, 74, and Marlon, 69, who all returned to Gary, their hometown, this week joined by their children and a packed crowd for the premiere. On the other side stand Randy and Janet Jackson, Michael’s closest siblings in age. Long considered the outlier in the family, Randy’s strained relations are partly rooted in one of the most personal betrayals in Jackson family history — when Jermaine married Alejandra Oaziaza, the mother of two of Randy’s children, Steven and Genevieve, in 1995. “There’s never been a doubt that the Jackson cousins have been closer than the Jackson siblings,” a source said.
articles.summary: 此新聞報導關於麥可傑克森傳記電影上映後，傑克森家族內部因電影和過往恩怨再次產生分歧。

articles.snippet: Boston Celtics star Jaylen Brown wins top honor for his...interviews? # Boston Celtics star Jaylen Brown wins top honor for his...interviews? Jaylen Brown has made a name for himself in NBA media circles for his thoughtful, and at times, unguarded answers about a variety of topics both on and off the court since joining the league in 2016. Brown was honored with a nod for the organization of journalists covering the sport for their First Team All-Interview. According to the PBWA, the designation, "which honors the players who are consistently thoughtful, insightful and engaging with the media," saw the Cal alum come in fifth overall in votes among those participating in the voting. San Antonio Spurs star Victor Wembanyama and Golden State Warriors legend Stephen Curry were the top two vote-getters, along with the rest of the First Team, Kevin Durant of the Houston Rockets, Donovan Mitchell of the Cleveland Cavaliers, and Brown.

articles.title: Billie Eilish Drops New ‘Hit Me Hard and Soft: The Tour’ Film Trailer - Billboard
articles.snippet: # Billie Eilish Drops New ‘Hit Me Hard and Soft: The Tour’ Film Trailer. The 10-time Grammy-, two-time Oscar-winning singer dropped a new trailer for her upcoming concert film *Hit Me Hard and Soft: The Tour (Live in 3D)* on Monday (April 13). The new three-minute-long trailer follows Eilish and three-time Oscar winner James Cameron as Eilish gets ready to perform for thousands of fans and Cameron prepares to capture the moment. On top of getting a look at how Cameron and Eilish filmed the singer’s concert, fans also get behind-the-scenes looks at how Eilish gets ready for a sold-out performance in the trailer. Her songs, including the Billboard Hot 100 No. 1 hit “Bad Guy” and “Chihiro,” are played over moments of Eilish on- and offstage as the words “Prepare for her vision / her words / her world” flash on the screen. *Hit Me Hard and Soft: The Tour (Live in 3D),* co-directed by Cameron and Eilish, hits theaters on May 8.
articles.summary: Billie Eilish Drops New ‘Hit Me Hard and Soft: The Tour’ Film Trailer - Billboard

articles.title: Kehlani On 'Folded,' Staying True To Her Moral Compass and More - Billboard
articles.snippet: Around the same time, Kehlani dropped “Folded,” the throwback slow jam that slowly grew into the biggest hit of her career, reaching No. 6 on the Billboard Hot 100 (her first top 10 on the chart) and eventually winning Grammys for best R&B song and best R&B performance. “You could think this is the furthest I’m ever going to go and this is the biggest it’s ever going to get,” says this year’s Women in Music Impact honoree, “and then God just surprises you and says, ‘No, I thought bigger for you always.’ ”. I really want some of these songs to make it into movies … and ultimately just have a really historical personal moment for me, and hopefully keep adding to the genre because that’s the coolest thing that “Folded” has done beyond anything — I’m watching the conversation change and I’m a part of it.

articles.snippet: # Kehlani Reveals Features on Self-Titled Album: Cardi B, Clipse, Usher, Lil Wayne, Missy Elliott & More. Ahead of Kehlani’s self-titled album’s arrival later this week, the Bay Area native revealed the decorated guests she’s recruited for collaborations on the project. The all-star lineup includes Missy Elliott, who appeared on Kehlani’s “Back and Forth” single, as well as Cardi B, Usher, Brandy, Clipse, Lil Wayne, Big Sean, Leon Thomas, Lil Jon and T-Pain. After notching her first Billboard Hot 100 top 10 hit with “Folded,” Kehlani is set to be honored with *Billboard*‘s Women In Music Impact award later this month. As far as goals for the eponymous LP, Kehlani hopes to perform with an orchestra at Manhattan’s famed Carnegie Hall and wants to see various tracks get placements in movies. “I want to do this album at Carnegie Hall with an orchestra,” she added. *Kehlani* will serve as the R&B singer’s fifth album. articles.title: Kanye West Postpones Marseille Concert After France Considers Banning Him From Entry - Variety
articles.snippet: # Kanye West Postpones Marseille Concert After France Considers Banning Him From Entry. Ye, the artist formerly known as Kanye West, took to X on Tuesday night to announce that he has postponed his upcoming concert in Marseille, after the office of French Interior Minister Laurent Nuñez confirmed it was exploring paths to ban him from performing in France’s oldest city. “After much thought and consideration, it is my sole decision to postpone my show in Marseille, France until further notice,” West wrote. Benoit Payan, the mayor of Marseille, also took a stand against West in an X post on March 4. While the threat was enough to get the 24-time Grammy winner to indefinitely push his show, actually banning West from performing in Marseille may have proved difficult. * ### Sean Baker’s ‘Anora’ Follow-Up ‘Ti Amo!’ Set at New Warner Bros. * ### ‘Marty Supreme’ Star Kevin O’Leary Defends Timothée Chalamet Amid Ballet and Opera Controversy: ‘He Took a Bum Rap on That’.

articles.title: New Prince Single “With This Tear” Released Ahead of Vault Project: Stream - consequence.net
articles.snippet: # New Prince Single “With This Tear” Released Ahead of Vault Project: Stream. The song is newly mixed and mastered by Grammy-nominated engineer and producer Chris James, who worked on Prince’s *HITnRUN Phase Two*, *Art Official Age*, and *Plectrumelectrum*. A new Prince recording, “With This Tear,” has been released from the artist’s legendarily filled musical vault through his NPG Records and Legacy Recordings. The single is the first release from a series of previously unreleased recordings leading up to a new vault album, according to Prince’s estate. “With This Tear” was put out a day before the 10th anniversary of Prince’s death and events at the musician’s famous estate and recording studio, Paisley Park, outside of Minneapolis. After writing the song, Prince offered it to singer Celine Dion, who recorded a version and released it on her 1992 self-titled album. ### Kacey Musgraves Releases New Song "Middle of Nowhere" Just Ahead of Coachella Performance: Stream. ### Boards of Canada Release First New Song in 13 Years: Stream.

articles.title: 'Michael' Review: Jaafar Jackson in Solid Michael Jackson Bio-Drama - The Hollywood Reporter
articles.snippet: ### Michael Jackson Biopic 'Michael': First Reactions](https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/michael-jackson-biopic-first-reactions-1236570185/). Those and other signs of Jackson’s enduring popularity suggest that diehard fans of his music — some of us used to wait hours for “Billie Jean” to come around again on MTV — will still seize the opportunity to celebrate the legendary streak that took him from childhood Motown stardom to pop royalty, with the three certified banger albums produced by Quincy Jones, *Off the Wall*, *Thriller* and *Bad*. What makes all this compelling is the degree to which Jaafar Jackson, the son of Michael’s older brother Jermaine, disappears into the role of his celebrated uncle. Even more than a decade later, when Michael’s solo stardom had far eclipsed that of his work with his brothers — let alone his earning power — Joe remained a controlling bully, insisting that his most famous son continue touring with the Jacksons, as they became known when Jermaine left to pursue a solo career with Motown.

articles.title: ‘Michael’ review: Michael Jackson biopic is painfully bad - New York Post
articles.snippet: # ‘Michael’ review: Michael Jackson biopic is painfully bad. How flabbergasting to see “Michael,” the new biopic about Michael Jackson, the best-selling solo artist of all time, crash and burn into such a cheap and embarrassing bore. Say what you will about Michael Jackson — and God knows there is plenty to say — he was not boring. In almost every moment of “Michael,” the King of Pop, played with panache and detail by his nephew Jaafar Jackson, is flashing a megawatt grin when he’s not staring longingly at his copy of “Peter Pan.” This Michael is perfect. That’s right — there is a scene in which Michael Jackson and Bubbles the chimp play Twister, underscored by “Blame It on the Boogie.”. In every sense, this is “Attorneys Present: The Michael Jackson Story.”. Jaafar Jackson plays his uncle Michael Jackson in "Michael." ©Lions Gate/Courtesy Everett Collection.

articles.snippet: ### Media News. # EXCLUSIVE: Complex Is Scaling Its Food Festival Family Style Into a Year-Round Media Brand. ## The streetwear brand is launching a slate of original programming for the brand while expanding its event footprint, projecting $20 million in revenue. Nearly two years after acquiring the food festival Family Style in May 2024, Complex is turning the brand into a full-fledged food media franchise. Now, the brand is extending into a year-round content operation, Complex chief executive Aaron Levant told ADWEEK. On Wednesday, the streetwear brand launched a dedicated YouTube channel for Family Style with four original series, an expansion that marks the most aggressive push into food from Complex since it was acquired by NTWRK in February 2024. Mark Stenberg is ADWEEK's senior media reporter. ## Media. #### YouTuber Jesser Launches Parent Company, Preps Business Expansion. Back in the U.S., West’s first live performances in five years at the SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles in early April drew between 60,000 to 80,000 people a night and reportedly earned $33M between them, leaving music and concert organizer execs in a conundrum over how to move on next with the artist.

articles.title: Spinelli Kilcollin Draws Jennie, Lizzo, Katseye and Kaytranada to L.A. Flagship Preview - WWD
articles.snippet: # Spinelli Kilcollin Draws Jennie, Lizzo, Katseye and Kaytranada to L.A. Flagship Preview. Spinelli Kilcollin, the Los Angeles-based fine jewelry brand known for its interconnected rings and architectural approach to design, offered a preview of its next chapter on Wednesday night. Founders Yves Spinelli and Dwyer Kilcollin hosted an after-hours gathering in a former gallery space soon to become the label’s 5,000-square-foot flagship at 747 North Western Avenue, cohosted with DJs and Simihaze Beauty founders, twins Simi and Haze Khadra. Spinelli Kilcollin has expanded in recent years from a design-focused jewelry studio into a broader cultural brand with growing retail ambitions and additional stores in New York’s SoHo shopping district as well as the Mayfair area of London. At the party, guests arrived via Waymo’s driverless fleet and walked through a series of installations, including floor-to-ceiling speaker walls, a mylar-wrapped dance floor and a metal-colored bounce house, as well as a claw machine stocked with Spinelli Kilcollin rings.

articles.title: Lady Gaga’s The Mayhem Ball Was One of the Biggest Pop Tours Ever: By the Numbers - Billboard
articles.snippet: Gaga's year of Mayhem grossed more than $400 million on tour. According to figures reported to Billboard Boxscore, Gaga grossed $419.5 million and sold just under two million tickets in her year of *Mayhem*. Dating to 2009 with shows in American clubs, Gaga has grossed more than $1.1 billion and sold 8.4 million tickets over 555 reported shows. The Mayhem Ball is Gaga’s highest-grossing tour, by a long shot. Not only is The Mayhem Ball Gaga’s biggest, it’s among the highest-grossing treks of the 2020s. The Mayhem Ball was not quite a series of mini-residencies in the way of Styles’ upcoming tour – Gaga played one show apiece in Antwerp, Brisbane, and Netherlands – but her runs of nine non-consecutive shows at Madison Square Garden, eight at Kia Forum, and five at Paris’ Accor Arena called fans to travel to major cities in lieu of individual stops in every corner of the U.S. or Europe.

articles.title: Review: Dancing around controversy, the empathetic 'Michael' bows out before getting dark - Los Angeles Times
articles.snippet: Jaafar Jackson in the movie “Michael.”. (Meanwhile, there’s no Janet, not even onscreen in the background or mentioned at all.) The Jacksons’ biological and, presumably, financial ties to the project would raise a World Cup stadium of red flags even without Jaafar Jackson, Michael’s nephew, playing the lead. But suffocating nepotism might be the only way for now to capture someone as singularly strange as Jackson — if Jaafar delivered a line reading that wasn’t note perfect, it’s likely that someone in his clan would pipe up and say so. Jackson’s music videos — “short films,” he preferred to call them — did that too, in abbreviation. After adult Michael is ordered to be the central cog in the Jacksons’ post-”Thriller” Victory tour, the film cuts to him on the couch watching his idol Charlie Chaplin work the assembly line in “Modern Times.” Alongside that, we catch glimpses of the megastar’s effect on his siblings, like concert scenes where the spotlight is only on him, or in Tito’s choice to wear a bedazzled hat with his name in all-caps.

articles.snippet: # Michael Jackson Biopic Cast Looks Shockingly Like The Real People They Play. ## The highly-anticipated "Michael" film, about the life and early career of Michael Jackson, hits theaters this weekend — and talk about what the film gets right and wrong has already reached a fever pitch. ## MJ is played by his nephew, Jaafar Jackson, and there are moments in the film when it's truly hard to tell them apart. ## With so much buzz circling Michael, let's take a look at some side-by-side comparisons of the actors and the real people they're portraying in the film. ## First up, there's Jaafar Jackson as Michael. Jaafar is the son of Jermaine Jackson, and makes a real splash of a debut in Michael. Universal Pictures | Michael Ochs Archives / Getty Images. articles.title: The Deals: Supertramp Bassist Dougie Thomson Sells Rights to MusicBird; Futures Raises $6M - Billboard
articles.snippet: Indie label collective **Futures Music Group**, founded by co-CEOs **Derek Davies** and **Dave Wallace**, raised $6 million from investors across the U.S. and U.K. The financing will allow the company to continue investing in infrastructure, artist development, operations and in-house technology development, according to a press release. Critically, this investment also allows the team at Futures to begin building out the next phase of the company, which includes expanding into the catalog space and development of our own internal technologies to help artists maximize value and fan experience across their releases,” added Wallace. Being listed once on Bandsintown automatically reaches core audiences everywhere and now powering concert listings on Amazon Music will extend artists’ reach to even more fans worldwide.”. “We are thrilled to be partnering with AKANDO Music for radio promotion and to begin with Chris Young’s latest single,” said Black River’s national director of promotion and artist relations, **Kellie LaJack**.

articles.title: Sitting in a jail cell, alone and hopeless, a man's life is suddenly changed - capradio.org
articles.snippet: # Sitting in a jail cell, alone and hopeless, a man's life is suddenly changed. Jay (not pictured) found himself alone and hopeless in a jail cell when a fellow inmate's unexpected words of comfort changed his life. And so I started making poor decisions," Jay said. Jay made a plan to end his life. "I wish I could tell you that I remember the [exact] words that he said to me, but what I remember is that his words landed with me, and instead of wanting my life to be over, suddenly I saw hope," Jay said. The interaction happened nearly ten years ago, but it was a pivotal moment in Jay's life, and one he thinks about all the time. Jay says that he wishes he could meet that man again and express his appreciation. To share the story of your unsung hero with the Hidden Brain team, record a voice memo on your phone and send it to myunsunghero@hiddenbrain.org.*.
articles.summary: Sitting in a jail cell, alone and hopeless, a man's life is suddenly changed - capradio.org

articles.title: Teddy Swims Talks Coachella, White Claw Collab And Friends Who Matter - Forbes
articles.snippet: He brought out Joe Jonas for a duet on the Jonas Brothers’ 2007 single “When You Look Me in the Eyes.” He welcomed Vanessa Carlton to the stage to sing — and accompany him on piano for — her 2002 smash “A Thousand Miles.” And there was legendary Van Halen frontman David Lee Roth, striding out to deliver their ’80s classic “Jump.” By Weekend 2, Roth was back for more and, in a full-circle gesture, Swims returned the favor on the final day of Coachella by popping on stage to perform “Lose Control” alongside fellow soul singer and past collaborator Givēon. “I love that White Claw also has this burden for real connection, bringing people together and it feels like we’re so aligned on just how do we keep things intimate and loving, caring about one another and being real connective,” Swims, whose favorite White Claw flavor is Ruby Grapefruit, says of the partnership.
articles.summary: Teddy Swims Talks Coachella, White Claw Collab And Friends Who Matter - Forbes

articles.title: Kehlani releases highly-anticipated self-titled album - The AU Review
articles.snippet: # Kehlani releases highly-anticipated self-titled album. **Kehlani** marks her birthday with the release of her fifth studio album, ***KEHLANI***, a project that feels deliberately stripped back to the essence of who she is as an artist. Self-titled albums often carry that weight of identity, and here it comes through in the emotional clarity of the songwriting – intimate, reflective, and unafraid to sit in discomfort. The album doesn’t reach outward so much as it settles inward, offering a clearer, more grounded reflection of an artist who knows exactly what she wants to say – and how she wants it to feel. * John Vincent III releases his much anticipated sophomore album, Songs…. * Tusks releases new single "Adore", and announces new album GOLD. * AYLA announces debut album Dopamine and releases a single to celebrate. * Georgia Lines releases her stunning debut album, The Rose of Jericho. * Ruel releases third single from his upcoming album Kicking My Feet.

articles.title: ‘Michael’ Heading Toward $85 Million-Plus Opening Weekend At Box Office - Yahoo News UK
articles.snippet: # ‘Michael’ Heading Toward $85 Million-Plus Opening Weekend At Box Office. The Michael Jackson biopic *Michael* is projected to earn more than $85 million in its opening weekend at the domestic box office. *Michael*, which stars Jaafar Jackson (Michael Jackson’s nephew/Jermaine Jackson’s son) in the title role, officially opened in North American theaters on Friday after special Wednesday night fan screenings and Thursday previews. As such, if a projection for its opening weekend by *Deadline* published on Friday night is any indication, Michael is taking a big first step toward its aspirations to make a sequel a reality. Lionsgate announced in an email Friday that *Michael* earned $3.8 million at fan screenings Wednesday night before playing in previews Thursday night, which resulted in a two-day domestic total of $12.6 million. Lionsgate also announced Friday that *Michael* also opened with $18.5 million in ticket sales in 82 international markets on Wednesday.

articles.title: Jada Pinkett Smith asks court to make Will Smith’s former friend pay her $49,000 legal bills - Los Angeles Times
articles.snippet: * Bilaal Salaam sued after airing podcast allegations about the Smiths’ private lives, which Pinkett Smith characterized as a “money shakedown” and part of a broader attempt to extort her. Jada Pinkett Smith is asking a judge to make Bilaal Salaam cover the $49,000 in legal fees she racked up fighting claims he made in a December lawsuit. According to a motion filed April 20 and obtained by The Times, Pinkett Smith is asking that Salaam pay $49,181.23, consisting of “reasonable attorneys’ fees incurred” in connection with Pinkett Smith’s successful special motion to strike Salaam’s complaint, “plus further fees and costs associated with this motion.”. Salaam’s suit claims that Pinkett Smith told him he would “end up missing or catch a bullet” if he kept “telling her personal business.” She also allegedly pressured him to sign a non-disclosure agreement. Pinkett Smith swiftly responded during an appearance on “The Breakfast Club” and said that Salaam started the rumors as part of a broader “money shakedown” and that his claims were “ridiculous and nonsense.”.

articles.title: The Legacy Of Michael Jackson Pondered Anew As ‘Michael’ Hits Theaters - Forbes
articles.snippet: “This is monumental,” said Gary, Indiana mayor Eddie Melton, taking to the red carpet as the new Michael Jackson biopic *Michael* premiered last week in the Steel City. “I’m so grateful to be able to be here,” said Jaafar Jackson, who portrays his uncle Michael in his acting debut and received the key to the city of Gary following a screening of the film. But he just carried himself with that grace and that humility,” said Michael’s oldest child (siblings ‘Bigi’ and Paris Jackson did not attend). “I think folks are starting to recognize that Gary is able to compete on a global scale,” said Mayor Melton. Dubbed “The First Lady of House Music,” Mazelle sang lead on Soul II Soul’s 1990 hit “Missing You.” Growing up down the street from the Jackson family in Gary, Mazelle now works extensively with kids in a music and mentorship role. “What Michael Jackson did so well was bring people together through his music,” said the producer.
articles.summary: 麥可傑克森傳記電影《Michael》在故鄉加里市首映，獲得市長、家人及當地社區的熱烈歡迎與高度讚揚，共同慶祝其音樂遺產與正面影響。

articles.snippet: The Dirty Nil are additionally slated to perform at the Burlington waterfront fest — operated by MRG Live — which will see over 30 acts performing between three music stages at Spencer Smith Park from June 20-21. Returning to their hometown, Walk off the Earth will bring their 2010s hits to the stage, including “Red Hands,” which hit No. 9 on the Billboard Canadian Hot 100, the band’s highest charting single in Canada. According to an initial concept — shared in a one-page brief in February — the festival includes three stages, including a main performance stage that’s filled with a lineup of hometown musicians from several music genres, with a “notable Canadian headliner” closing out each evening. The Lakeshore Music & Arts Festival will maintain Sound of Music’s free ticket structure, but will implement paid VIP ticket tiers to “enhance the visitor experience.” The brief outlined that the planned VIP section features premium seating, exclusive food and drink options, merchandise and artist meet-and-greet experiences.

articles.title: Kehlani’s self-titled album tracks ranked from worst to best - Rolling Out
articles.snippet: # Kehlani’s self-titled album tracks ranked from worst to best. Oakland R&B star blends nostalgia, vocals, and legacy collabs in a genre-shaping album. Kehlani’s self-titled album arrives after a decade of steady growth from mixtape standout to mainstream R&B force. The album draws heavily from 1990s and 2000s R&B, not as imitation but as reference point. Jimmy Jam and Terry Lewis contribute to the album’s more classic R&B textures, while other tracks lean into contemporary polish. Big Sean)** – Smooth but relatively understated mid-album moment. Usher)** – Classic R&B breakup exchange with polished delivery. Cardi B)** – Lively collaboration with strong mainstream appeal. Folded** – The project’s defining hit and emotional anchor. Unlearn** – Gospel-leaning closer that ties the album’s emotional themes together. By the final track, Kehlani reads less like a diary and more like a reflection on R&B itself. * album, charts, Collaboration, Folded, Kehlani, Music, Pop, ranking, review, rnb.

articles.title: Kehlani’s new self-titled project is a no-skip album — Album Review - MELODIC Magazine
articles.snippet: ### Kehlani’s new self-titled project is a no-skip album — Album Review. # Kehlani’s new self-titled project is a no-skip album — Album Review. It’s followed by her biggest track yet, “Folded” — the single earning Best R&B Song and Best R&B Performance at this years Grammys speaks for itself. “I Need You” featuring Brandy is arguably one of the top three on the album, and it’s because she manages to bring back the Princess of R&B and also blend in a modern sound and production that feels like a ode to the genre overall. Three more solo tracks, “You Got It,” “Call Me Back,” and “Still” are all heartfelt, vulnerable and reflective to the new, mature sound Kehlani carries throughout this album in comparison to her past projects. Kehlani and Cardi B together create some of the best hip-hop/R&B songs, just like on Cardi’s newest album with “Safe.” We’re hoping for more future collaborations between the two. Kehlani outdid herself with this album, showcasing her true love as a fan and artist of R&B.

articles.title: 'We have no sleep': What it's like to run a round-the-clock celebrity fan page - BBC
articles.snippet: "I feel like we have no sleep - we're at this 24 hours a day, seven days a week," says 25-year-old Canadian, Jay. He's a researcher for the social media account Buzzing Pop, which has 250,000 followers on X and posts round-the-clock news and gossip about the biggest celebrities in the world. "We're always keeping up - it's crazy but we love it," says Jay. Jay is part of a new generation of social media creators whose job it is to provide constant updates on fans' favourite film, TV and music stars. He says he started his fan account to "feel a bit closer" to the star, adding "I got likes from Taylor Swift, follows from her dancers and her team". And while Buzzing Pop is not dedicated to a single celebrity or music artist, Jay says his team works hard to keep their posts "neutral and informative".

articles.title: Beloved ABC host and rock musician dead at 64 after assisted suicide: ‘Did it his way’ - Yahoo
articles.snippet: # Beloved ABC host and rock musician dead at 64 after assisted suicide: ‘Did it his way’. James Valentine, a popular ABC radio and television host for more than 30 years and a rock musician, has died. Valentine died by assisted suicide at home two years after he was diagnosed with cancer, **according to multiple reports.**. “Throughout his illness, James did it his way, which lasted all the way until the day when he made the choice to do voluntary assisted dying,” the statement continued. He was a saxophonist, and his music career took off in 1982 when he was a member of Jo Jo Zep. He also played with Model from 1984 to 1987 and Absent Friends from 1989 to 1990. Valentine hosted radio and television shows for ABC for nearly 40 years. He worked for 702 ABC Sydney for more than 25 years before retiring because of his illness. articles.title: Ex-Warriors star Klay Thompson accused of cheating off the court - New York Post
articles.snippet: # Ex-Warriors star Klay Thompson accused of cheating off the court. Klay Thompson has been accused of cheating … again, and this time there’s no defensive scheme a coach can draw up to help him. Thompson, now with the Mavericks and a former four-time NBA champion with the Warriors, found himself at the center of a storm Saturday after musician Megan Thee Stallion, whom he has been in a relationship with since last summer, publicly accused him of cheating on her. It wasn’t just the accusation — which is not the first time Thompson has been accused of infidelity — it was the tone. Megan famously spent holidays with Thompson and his family. Megan repeatedly showed up courtside to Thompson’s games. Thompson was most recently spotted with his mother, Julie, on April 14 at Megan’s Broadway performance of “Moulin Rouge.”. In a statement to our very own Page Six, Megan said it was her decision to end the relationship with Thompson. Thompson has yet to respond to Megan’s claims.

articles.title: Jay’s imprint clear as Cards’ outfield, baserunning stay sharp - MLB.com
articles.snippet: For Jay, the trip to Miami added a personal element, but his influence continues to show in how the Cardinals prepare and play. “It’s always good to come back home,” Jay said. From pregame work to in-game execution, Jay has helped shape how the Cardinals’ outfielders and baserunners operate, influencing both their positioning in the field and awareness on the bases. The Cardinals recorded an outfield assist during the game when Nathan Church threw from right field to third base, helping erase Javier Sanoja in a rundown, the third of the series. “That’s a big credit to [Jay], working with him on base running and stuff like [where to position myself],” Walker said. “Talking to [Jay], like every four at-bats, he just reminds me of what the pitcher does, how quick he is to the plate, what the situation is, to kind of keep it running for my brain, so I can just have in the back of my mind so I can, you know, take advantage of the situation if need be.”. “The biggest thing is this game is not easy, and you're gonna make mistakes,” Jay said.
